items.title: Critical RCE and other vulnerabilities in 25 Amasty Magento extensions
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability leading to remote code execution and other medium severity issues. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. CISA added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 3, 2026, due to active exploitation.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce (Multiple Vulnerabilities including Auth Bypass)
items.summary: Adobe released a security update addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. This includes CVE-2026-34646, an incorrect authorization flaw allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique. Attacks have been observed against a significant percentage of stores, though Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, older versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source to address multiple vulnerabilities. These could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce (including Stored XSS)
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical and important vulnerabilities, including security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution. Specifically, CVE-2025-47110 addresses a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability via server-side template injection.

items.title: SessionReaper: Unauthenticated RCE in Magento & Adobe Commerce
items.summary: SessionReaper, a critical vulnerability (CVSS 9.1), allows customer account takeover and unauthenticated remote code execution under certain conditions. Automated attacks have been observed globally, with many stores still vulnerable despite an emergency fix released by Adobe in September 2025.
